revision.text:
[[ja:伊去波夜和気命神社]] {{Expand Japanese|伊去波夜和気命神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine | native_name = 伊去波夜和気命神社 | image = Haiden of Ikohayawake-no-mikoto-jinja shrine.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|38.415389|141.362972}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|az|Sarutahiko|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|fa|ساروتاهیکو اوکامی|fr|Sarutahiko Ōkami|it|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|サルタヒコ|ko|사루타히코 오카미|mni|ꯁꯔꯨꯇꯍꯤꯀꯣ ꯑꯣꯀꯃꯤ|pl|Sarutahiko|ru|Сарутахико|simple|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ta|சருதாகிகோ அகாமி|tr|Sarutahiko Ōkami|zh|猿田彥|qid=Q3090037|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine'''|伊去波夜和気命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いこはやわけのみことじんじゃ * イサリハヤワケノ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō * イコ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Isarihayawakeno- Shrine|lt=Isarihayawakeno- Shrine|qid=Q135040003|1=Isarihayawakeno- Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '宮城県石巻市大宮町37', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '宮城県石巻市渡波', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊去波夜和気命神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11379181}} == Japanese content == '''Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いこはやわけ（わき）のみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ishinomaki City|Q270814|qid=Q270814|1=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|qid=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. It is one of the shrines listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as a single seat among the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. Within Ishinomaki City, there are two shrines that are considered candidate sites (ronsha). === Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine (Watanoha Ōmiya) === {{Shrine |Name=Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine |Image=[[File:Haiden of Ikohayawake-no-mikoto-jinja shrine.JPG|250px|Haiden of Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine after the earthquake]]
Haiden |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|qid=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ishinomaki City|Q270814|qid=Q270814|1=Ishinomaki}} Ōmiyachō 37 |Coordinates={{WikiCoordinates|38|24|55.4|N|141|21|46.7|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark}} |Deities={{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
{{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286}}
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}
{{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} ({{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}) |ShrineRank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded=Unknown |AnnualFestival={{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|qid=Q2502|1=April 10}} |Map={{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Ikohayawake
no-Mikoto Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} === Enshrined Deities === * A total of five deities are enshrined: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}, {{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, and {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} ({{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}). It is said that the collective name of these deities is '''“Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto.”''' === History === The date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. Originally, the shrine buildings were located elsewhere, but they were transferred to the present Watanoha Ōmiya location in the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|qid=Q1195131|1=Kansei}}. Among the 100 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} in Mutsu Province listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, this shrine is known by the names “Hama Daimyōjin” and “Shiromori Myōjin.” Today it is commonly called “Myōjinsha” and is worshipped as a deity of abundant fishing, maritime safety, and salt production. Among the enshrined deities, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto (considered identical to {{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji-no-Kami}}), Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, and Futsunushi-no-Mikoto are also deities of {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|qid=Q133753|1=Shiogama shrine}}, the Ichinomiya of Ōshū. Amaterasu Ōmikami and Ukanomitama-no-Kami (Toyouke Ōkami) are the deities of the Inner and Outer Shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|qid=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. On March 11, 2011, the tsunami caused by the {{ill|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Q36204|qid=Q36204|1=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}} inflicted devastating damage on the Watanoha area where Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine is located. The subsidiary shrines, shrine office, and the chief priest’s residence were damaged; however, people who evacuated to the shrine’s haiden, which stands slightly elevated and surrounded by a sacred forest, were unharmed. Since then, thanks to the efforts of volunteers and parishioners, recovery has progressed with the shrine at the center of the community’s collective efforts. === Precincts (After the Earthquake) === * Within the approach and precincts are an ema hall, a sumo ring for dedication matches, stone shrines, and other features. File:Torii gate of Ikohayawake-no-mikoto-jinja shrine after massive earthquake.JPG|Near the torii gate of Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine
The torii and other structures were washed away by the tsunami File:Precincts of Ikohayawake-no-mikoto-jinja shrine.JPG|Precincts of Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine File:Dousojin of Ikohayawake-no-mikoto-jinja shrine.JPG|Wood-carved Dōsojin * Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Haiden of Ikohayawake-no-mikoto-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Haiden of Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine File:Haiden of Ikoahaywake-no-mikoto-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Haiden of Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine File:Honden of Ikohayawake-no-mikoto-jinja shrine.JPG|Honden of Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine * Subsidiary shrines ** Yakumo Shrine (Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}) ** Ujigami Shrine (Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|qid=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}) ** In addition, behind the main shrine buildings stands a Soreisha (ancestral spirit shrine) donated in March 2013 by the Kyoto East Lions Club for the repose of the souls of those who perished in the Great East Japan Earthquake. File:Yakumo-jinja shrine at Ikohayawake-no-mikoto-jinja shrine.JPG|Yakumo Shrine File:Ujigamisha shrine of Ikohayawake-no-mikoto-jinja shrine.JPG|Ujigami Shrine File:Soreisha of Ikohayawake-no-mikoto-jinja shrine.JPG|Soreisha of Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine File:Soreisha of Ikohayawake-no-mikoto-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Painting of Izanagi and Izanami in the haiden of the Soreisha === Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine (Mizunuma, Ishinomaki City) === {{Shrine |Name=Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine |Image={{Image requested|cat=Ishinomaki City}} |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|qid=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ishinomaki City|Q270814|qid=Q270814|1=Ishinomaki}} Mizunuma, Aza Hirahata 164 |Coordinates={{WikiCoordinates|38|28|52.7|N|141|22|14.1|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark}} |Deity={{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji-no-Mikoto}} |ShrineRank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor), candidate shrine |Founded=Unknown |AnnualFestival=Formerly {{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|qid=Q2788|1=August 1}} |Map={{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Ikohayawake
no-Mikoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|38|28|52.7|N|141|22|14.1|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|38|28|52.7|N|141|22|14.1|E}}}} }} === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji-no-Mikoto}} === History === Both the founding and history of this shrine are unknown. One theory holds that it was founded in the 15th year of the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} era, and it is regarded as a candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine. === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} === External Links === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310030822 Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine] ({{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|qid=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}} of Miyagi Prefecture) {{Shinto}} {{-}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ikohayawake-no-Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:石巻市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ishinomaki, Miyagi]]
